text: Web security is essential for building safe websites. First, use HTTPS to encrypt data between the user's browser and the server. This prevents attackers from stealing sensitive information like passwords or credit card details. Second, implement proper authentication. Strong password policies, two-factor authentication, and secure session management help verify user identities and block unauthorized access. Third, always validate user input. Never trust data from forms or URLs; check its length, format, and content. Without validation, common vulnerabilities like SQL injection or cross-site scripting (XSS) can occur. These attacks allow hackers to manipulate databases or inject malicious code. By following these practices, developers can significantly reduce security risks and protect both users and data.
